name: Chongqing
intro_vibe: The 8D Vertical Metropolis: A labyrinth of fog, mountains, and neon where ground levels are illusions and navigation requires three-dimensional logic.

general_info.description: Chongqing is a massive megacity built on rugged terrain at the confluence of the Yangtze and Jialing rivers. Known as the '8D City,' its infrastructure defies traditional mapping; roads spiral over buildings and monorails pass through residential towers. It acts as a high-density 'living laboratory' where the ground floor is fluid and elevators function as vital public transit.

etiquette_tips: Digital Pre-configuration: Cash is rare; configure WeChat and Alipay with an international card before landing.
Navigation Reality: 2D maps fail here. Distance is meaningless without elevation data. Use Amap (Gaode).
Hotpot Thermal Zones: The Jiugongge grid isn't for different broths. Use the center for blanching and corners for long stewing.
Dipping Sauce Rule: Strictly Sesame Oil + Minced Garlic. It acts as a fire extinguisher for the capsaicin.
Loudness ≠ Aggression: Locals speak at a high volume. Shouting in restaurants is enthusiastic social energy.
Respect the Monarchy of Offal: Tripe (Maodu) and Duck Intestine are the prizes. Refusing them is okay; mocking them is rude.
Face Management: Do not use flash on monorail platforms; it distracts the drivers.
Bang Bang Army: Negotiate the price (¥10-20) before porters carry your luggage up stairs.
Ganbei Hospitality: Bottoms up drinking is an intense social ritual. Refusal requires a medical excuse.
TCM Cooling: In summer heat, drink Suan Mei Tang (Sour Plum Soup) or Lu Dou Tang (Mung Bean Soup) to detoxify 'heatiness'.

neighborhoods.name: Jiefangbei (Yuzhong)
neighborhoods.description: High-density commercial core on a hilly peninsula. Best for first-timers wanting proximity to major sights.

neighborhoods.name: Nanbin Road (Nan'an)
neighborhoods.description: Located across the Yangtze. Offers the best skyline views. Quieter and upscale.

neighborhoods.name: Guanyinqiao (Jiangbei)
neighborhoods.description: Modern commercial center and nightlife hub. Preferred by locals for shopping.

neighborhoods.name: Eling & Testbed 2
neighborhoods.description: Hipster enclave atop a ridge converted from old currency printing factories.

logistics.title: Getting Around
logistics.summary: Master the vertical infrastructure to survive the terrain.
details: CRT Metro: Line 2 is for sightseeing; Line 3 is for rush hour endurance.
Yellow Ferraris: Bright yellow taxis known for F1-style aggressive mountain maneuvering.
Vertical Transit: The Crown Escalator and Yangtze Cableway are vital arteries for workers.
Navigation Tools: Use Amap (Gaode) for staircases and altitude-aware routing.

logistics.title: Apps & Payments
logistics.summary: The city operates on an invisible digital web.
details: Identity & Pay: WeChat and Alipay are required for ordering food and metro payments.
Mini-Programs: Use WeChat mini-programs to bypass queues for the cableway.
Dead Zones: Expect loss of signal in old vertical blocks. Screenshot routes.

description.short: Spicy beef tallow broth divided into thermal zones for precision cooking.
description.history: A communal ritual rooted in the city's history as a river port.
description.insider_tip: The 'Up and Down Seven Times' rule is used for blanching tripe in the center square.

description.short: A breakfast staple defined by its precise balance of numbing chili oil.
description.history: Chongqing's daily fuel, traditionally eaten on the sidewalk at 'Stool Noodles'.
description.insider_tip: Order 'Gan Liu' (dry) for more intense flavor.

description.short: Translucent sweet potato starch noodles in a heavy vinegar and chili broth.
description.history: A street food icon, most famous at Hao You Lai in Jiefangbei.
description.insider_tip: Chewy and elastic; avoid broth splash-back.

description.short: Savory rice flour porridge topped with fried dough twists (sanzi), chili oil, and scallions.
description.history: A traditional breakfast art form now found in older residential quarters.
description.insider_tip: Mix the crispy toppings into the porridge quickly before they get soggy.

description.short: A clear jelly served cold with brown sugar, hawthorn, and raisins. The primary 'fire extinguisher'.
description.history: Traditional summer dessert made from Nicandra plant seeds.
description.insider_tip: The ultimate relief after a spicy hotpot session.

description.short: Hand-pounded rice cakes, deep-fried and dipped in brown sugar syrup and soybean powder.
description.history: Classic snack served as a sweet offset to spicy meals.
description.insider_tip: Best eaten hot while the center is still gooey.

description.short: A monorail station passing through the 19th floor of a residential high-rise.
description.history: Constructed simultaneously with the building to maximize space.
description.insider_tip: View from the street level platform at 7 AM for the best shots.

description.short: 11-story stilt house complex built into a cliff. Cyberpunk aesthetic.
description.history: Modern reconstruction of traditional stilt houses.
description.insider_tip: Best viewed at night from Qiansimen Bridge.

description.short: Residential complex with no elevators and multi-level road exits.
description.history: A purest expression of Chongqing's lived verticality.
description.insider_tip: Exits on Levels 1, 10, and 15 all lead to different streets.

description.short: Hyper-modern complex with a 250m high horizontal skybridge.
description.history: Symbolizes Chongqing’s repositioning as a global megacity.
description.insider_tip: A visual anchor for understanding the scale of the river confluence.

description.short: UNESCO site with thousands of religious and secular carvings.
description.history: Dates from 9th–13th centuries; depicts morality and family ethics.
description.insider_tip: A full-day trip 100km west; provides historical depth to the city.

description.short: Epic landscape of natural stone bridges, sinkholes, and gorges.
description.history: UNESCO natural site used as a reset from urban density.
description.insider_tip: Best treated as an overnight trip for relief from the summer heat.

description.short: Qing-era complex built by migrant merchants from southern China.
description.history: Provides insight into the migration that shaped Chongqing.
description.insider_tip: Low-crowd site offering architectural calm near the riverside.

itinerary.title: Vertical Calibration
itinerary.description: Start at the Crown Escalator. Gain 52m of elevation in 3 minutes to recalibrate your intuition of multi-level streets.

itinerary.title: Xiao Mian Fuel
itinerary.description: Find a sidewalk 'Fly Restaurant'. Order Gan Liu Xiao Mian with Wan Za topping to fuel the afternoon.

itinerary.title: Infrastructure Safari
itinerary.description: Ride CRT Line 2 to Liziba Station. Watch the train pass through the building, then explore the elevator-less White Elephant Complex.

itinerary.title: The Hotpot Institution
itinerary.description: Dine at a Jiugongge hotpot spot. Master the grid zones and fireproof your stomach with sesame oil dipping sauce.

itinerary.title: Neon Skyline
itinerary.description: Walk across Qiansimen Bridge at dusk. View Hongya Cave exterior against the CBD skyline to understand the city's neon-lit scale.

affiliate_products.title: High-Traction Walking Shoes
affiliate_products.reason: Non-negotiable for thousands of steep stairs and vertical hills daily.

affiliate_products.title: Portable Neck Fan
affiliate_products.reason: Crucial for surviving high 'wet bulb' temperatures of the 'Furnace' summer.

affiliate_products.title: High-Capacity Power Bank
affiliate_products.reason: Logistical failure occurs if your phone dies, as payments and navigation are 100% digital.
